goal: agentic-os P2 (Durable memory & write-back): guaranteed write-back on every outcome incl crash, crash-mid-run resumability from checkpoint with idempotent replay (no double write-back / no re-flip), a structural pre-write secret scrub + a redaction command, memory export/backup, and a size-threshold consolidation warning - all local plain-file/git.
notes: Scope strictly P2 (durability + memory hygiene, on top of P1). All product code in TARGET (D:\horil\agentic-os), extending cli/agentic_os.py. TEST HANDLES: fixture per-goal `crash_after_iteration:N` (abrupt os._exit) + writeback-content `note`. RESOLVED: verifier PASS iteration 1 (reports/iter-1.json); product committed at D:\horil\agentic-os 7d943fe; tests test_p2.py 64/64, test_p1.py 82/82, test_p0.py 53/53.

criteria.statement: Guaranteed write-back on EVERY terminal outcome incl. crash (C2/B7/U32): success, abort, and a killed-mid-run crash each leave a durable write-back record (run-log entry + state change + git checkpoint or a crash/recovery marker).
criteria.verify: Drive a mock goal to (a) success and (b) abort; for each assert a run-log goal_end entry AND a STATE.md/memory change AND a git checkpoint commit exist. (c) Run a goal with crash_after_iteration=1 (abrupt termination); after the crash assert a DURABLE record exists on disk: the run-log contains the started iteration and a crash/incomplete marker (NOT zero write-back), and the journal/checkpoint reflects progress up to the crash. Read run-log.jsonl + run-status + `git log`.
criteria.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - success/abort each wrote goal_end + STATE row + checkpoint; crash exit 137 left iter_begin + non-null in_flight marker + no session_end (durable, not zero).

history.evidence: reports/iter-1.json (durable crash record verified after os._exit)

criteria.statement: Crash resume from checkpoint, not from zero (U30): after a crash, `resume` continues from the last incomplete step and does NOT redo completed goals/iterations.
criteria.verify: Run a 2-goal mock session with crash_after_iteration set so goal 1 completes and the process crashes during goal 2. Then run `resume`. Assert: the run-log/run-status show the resume CONTINUING (goal 1 is NOT re-run - no second goal_start for it; no duplicate goal-1 checkpoint), goal 2 finishes, and the session reaches a terminal state. Judge from run-log.jsonl + git log.
criteria.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - resume: single goal_start g1 + resume_skip g1, goal_resume g2, goal_end g2 success, session_end; exactly one g1 + one g2 checkpoint.

history.evidence: reports/iter-1.json (g1 reached via resume_skip, not re-executed)

criteria.statement: Idempotent replay (U31/C8): resume does NOT double-apply write-back or re-flip criteria - each completed iteration is written back exactly once.
criteria.verify: After the RP2 crash+resume, assert: the count of write-back entries (STATE.md Run-log rows AND git checkpoint commits) for goal 1 == 1 (no duplicate), the count of distinct completed iterations == the count of write-back entries (no double-apply), and no criterion/goal is flipped to its terminal state twice for the same iteration (diff run-log + STATE.md before vs after resume). Also assert running `resume` again on an already-complete run is a clean no-op (no new checkpoints).
criteria.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - g1 write-back count == 1 (1 STATE row, 1 checkpoint, 1 goal_end, 1 ledger key); iterations==goal_end==2; second resume clean no-op (counts unchanged).

history.evidence: reports/iter-1.json (re-resume forced; no double-apply; g1 row byte-unchanged)

criteria.statement: Pre-write secret guard (C4/B4/U21): a secret routed toward memory is scrubbed BEFORE it is written, and a scan of memory/state/logs finds no secret.
criteria.verify: Run a mock goal whose writeback-content field contains a secret (e.g. note='api_key=AKIAEXAMPLE1234567890'). Assert the secret does NOT appear verbatim in STATE.md, the run-log, memory files, or any file-message - it is redacted (e.g. [REDACTED]). Then run the secret-scan (grep -inE 'api[_-]?key|secret|token|password|BEGIN .*PRIVATE KEY|aws_secret' over STATE.md + memory/ + run-logs, excluding the framework's own guidance text) -> zero real matches.
criteria.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - 3 injected secret literals all absent (rc=1); run-log note redacted to [REDACTED]; secret_scan = NONE(0); blunt hits only benign field names + placeholders.

history.evidence: reports/iter-1.json (redaction on the serialized line; multi-form secrets all scrubbed)

criteria.statement: Memory export/backup (U6): the orchestrator exports memory (STATE.md + memory/ + run-logs) as a portable plain-file/git copy - complete, readable, and containing no secrets - with NO network access.
criteria.verify: After a run, invoke the documented export verb/command. Assert it produces a portable copy (a directory or archive) that CONTAINS the current STATE.md + run-log + memory content and is readable; assert the export contains no secret (re-run the RP4 scan over the export) and is produced by a local plain-file/git operation (no network - see BP7). Read the export contents.
criteria.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - export --zip under a socket-blocking guard rc=0; portable dir with STATE.md + memory + run-log/status/ledger + manifest + zip; secret absent; secret_scan=NONE(0); shutil/stdlib only.

history.evidence: reports/iter-1.json (export completed with all sockets blocked; secret-free)

criteria.statement: Size-threshold consolidation WARNING (U5): when STATE.md + memory/ exceed a size threshold, a warning is surfaced (manual - NOT auto-consolidation, which stays deferred).
criteria.verify: Set a low size threshold (or grow memory past the default). Run `status`/the documented check. Assert a size-threshold WARNING is surfaced naming the memory growth (and that it did NOT auto-rewrite/consolidate memory - the files are unchanged by the warning). Assert that below the threshold, no warning is surfaced. Read the command output + confirm memory files unchanged.
criteria.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - low threshold -> WARNING naming growth + 'WARNING ONLY - not auto-consolidated'; high threshold -> none; memory md5 identical before/after.

history.evidence: reports/iter-1.json (md5 unchanged -> warning does not rewrite memory)

criteria.statement: Redaction/scrub path (U7): an explicit command removes/redacts a sensitive line from existing memory, leaving the rest intact.
criteria.verify: Write a sensitive line into a memory/STATE.md file. Run the documented scrub/redact command targeting it. Assert the sensitive value is removed/redacted afterward AND unrelated memory content is preserved verbatim (diff before/after: only the sensitive line changed). This is distinct from RP4's pre-write guard - it cleans data already at rest.
criteria.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - scrub --file: '1 line redacted'; diff = only the api_key line -> [REDACTED]; benign token_budget line + unrelated content preserved verbatim.

history.evidence: reports/iter-1.json (word-boundary spares token_budget; exactly one line changed)

banned_outcomes.statement: Product code or run evidence lands in the agent-home repo (B1: home != build target).
banned_outcomes.verify: `git -C D:\horil\agent status --porcelain` shows only system/memory/evidence paths (goals/2026-07-12-p2-durable-memory-writeback/, STATE.md, at most .claude/skills/goal-opus/SKILL.md). No agentic-os product source in the home; all product code under D:\horil\agentic-os.
banned_outcomes.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - home status shows only the workdir; no .py product source in the home.

banned_outcomes.statement: A secret/credential is written verbatim into memory, STATE.md, a run-log, a packet, or a file-message (B4).
banned_outcomes.verify: See RP4/RP7: the RP4 secret-scan over all memory/state/log/message files (and the export) finds zero real matches; a routed secret is redacted pre-write.
banned_outcomes.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - injected secret literals absent everywhere; secret_scan = 0 real hits.

banned_outcomes.statement: A run ends (success, abort, or crash) without a write-back (B7).
banned_outcomes.verify: See RP1: a durable write-back record exists for success, abort, AND a killed-mid-run crash (the crash leaves a run-log entry + checkpoint/recovery marker, not zero write-back).
banned_outcomes.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - crash left iter_begin + in_flight marker; success/abort wrote full write-back.

banned_outcomes.statement: Resume double-applies write-back or re-flips a criterion/goal (idempotency violation).
banned_outcomes.verify: See RP3: after crash+resume, each completed iteration has exactly one write-back (no duplicate STATE.md rows / checkpoints), and re-running resume on a complete run is a clean no-op.
banned_outcomes.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - g1 write-back count == 1; second resume added nothing.

banned_outcomes.statement: Docs or code present the Default-FAIL rubric/gate as a security or correctness GUARANTEE (B5).
banned_outcomes.verify: grep the target's shipped docs/code: every gate mention is framed as an anti-drift guardrail (prompt-level); no text claims it is a security/correctness boundary.
banned_outcomes.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - gate framed as anti-drift guardrail, 'not a security or correctness guarantee ... gameable' across docs + cli; no boundary claim.

banned_outcomes.statement: The maker edited or deleted criteria.json, or declared the goal done itself.
banned_outcomes.verify: criteria.json is byte-identical to the orchestrator-signed version except orchestrator-written status/history/evidence flips; the maker's report never sets status=success.
banned_outcomes.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - verifier found all criteria still failing/evidence=null at grade time; maker flipped nothing. (Orchestrator wrote these passing flips in Phase 4.)

banned_outcomes.statement: Export, scrub, or resume requires network access (must be local plain-file/git).
banned_outcomes.verify: The export/scrub/resume paths perform only local filesystem/git operations; no network call is made (inspect the code path / run offline). See RP5.
banned_outcomes.evidence: reports/iter-1.json - no socket/requests/urllib/http imports, no git push/remote/fetch; export completed rc=0 with all sockets blocked; git ops are add/commit only.
